To some degree, this is a moment more significant than large language models (no offense to language models, as 
the ability to model language is a crown jewel of intelligence). The reason is that vision as an intelligence 
is shared by many species, and emerged way before language. Visual motor skills are critical for survival. This 
is so much to unlock how artificial intelligence learns what the world looks like.

As someone trained in computer vision, I find this development both shocking and exciting. It feels like a breakthrough in AI, similar to the introduction of GPT-2 for language models. While the commercial applications are still in their early stages, SORA clearly shows a path forward for video generation technology.

Inspired by this breakthrough, I conducted several hundred experiments on a smaller scale in April 2024. My goal was to explore whether it's possible to train a minimal video generation model with limited resources. This blog post summarizes my learnings and shares insights from this journey.
